prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with Cloud Storage enabled.
A GCS bucket to use as the data lake target (gcs_bucket_name).
A GCP location/region for the bucket and catalog (gcp_location, e.g. us, us-central1, or eu).
A warehouse_location: the root GCS path for Iceberg data (e.g. gs://my-bucket/warehouse).
A namespace and main_branch_name (Iceberg branch, commonly main) for the destination tables.
A choice of Iceberg catalog_type: BigLake or Polaris.
For the BigLake catalog: a GCP service account with Storage Admin, BigQuery Data Editor, BigQuery User, and Service Usage Consumer roles, plus its downloaded JSON key.
For the Polaris catalog: an OAuth client_id and client_secret obtained when creating a principal in Polaris.

authMethods.name: Service account JSON key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to GCS (and the BigLake catalog) using the service_account_json field, which holds the complete JSON content of a GCP service account key file. The service account must be granted Storage Admin for full bucket access, plus BigQuery Data Editor, BigQuery User, and Service Usage Consumer for BigLake catalog operations.

authMethods.name: Polaris OAuth client credentials
authMethods.summary: When catalog_type is Polaris, authenticate the connector to the Polaris catalog server using the OAuth catalog_type.client_id and catalog_type.client_secret received when creating a principal in Polaris.

setupSteps.title: Create a GCS bucket and warehouse path
setupSteps.body: In your Google Cloud project, create a Cloud Storage bucket in the desired region. Note the bucket name for gcs_bucket_name and the GCP location for gcp_location. Decide the root path Iceberg data should be written to and set it as warehouse_location (e.g. gs://my-bucket/warehouse).

setupSteps.title: Create a service account and download its JSON key
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console go to IAM and Admin > Service Accounts, create a service account in your project, then create a key of type JSON and download the key file. Paste the full contents of that file into the service_account_json field. If gcp_project_id is left empty it is taken from the service account.

setupSteps.title: Grant the required IAM roles
setupSteps.body: Grant the service account Storage Admin for full GCS bucket access. For a BigLake catalog also grant BigQuery Data Editor (catalog operations), BigQuery User (BigQuery operations), and Service Usage Consumer (using GCP services).

setupSteps.title: Choose and configure the Iceberg catalog
setupSteps.body: Set catalog_type to BigLake or Polaris. For BigLake, the service account JSON above is sufficient. For Polaris, create a principal in Polaris and supply the resulting OAuth credentials as catalog_type.client_id and catalog_type.client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Set namespace and branch, then save
setupSteps.body: Set namespace (the default namespace for tables, e.g. default) and main_branch_name (the Iceberg branch, e.g. main). Optionally set gcp_project_id and a gcs_endpoint override if needed, then save and test the connection.
